title: 70-lesson milestone: speed gate
description: Speed gate at lesson 70 — must hold 70 WPM to pass
text: Seventy lessons. Numbers do not lie — your fingers move faster than ever. The keyboard is now an extension of your thoughts, not an obstacle. Keep training; the last thirty lessons will turn this speed into automatic, lifelong muscle memory.

tips.text: Seventy lessons. Count them. That is steady work, and it shows in your hands.
Hold seventy words a minute here and you have earned the word fast. No flattery in it - just the figure.

tips.target: Seventy lessons. Numbers do not lie — your fingers move faster than ever. The keyboard is now an extension of

tips.text: Thirty lessons left. Keep showing up and they will take you well past this. Consistency does the work, not heroics.

tips.target: your thoughts, not an obstacle. Keep training; the last thirty lessons will turn this speed into automatic, lifelong muscle memory.

character_tips.anna: Seventy words a minute, sustained. That is the mark of someone who can be handed real work. Take the moment, then carry on. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Seventy is where typing stops being a chore and starts being a tool. Plenty of able writers never reach it. You have. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Don't celebrate the number. Notice the calm in your hands - that is what you actually built. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Good. Quietly, properly good. Now keep the rhythm even and let the speed look after itself. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
